cases.title: Scenario matrix row for Attempt/audit coverage persists success and failure CheckInAttempt rows
preconditions: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, FR-30, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18, AC-19).
Open class session exists with enrolled student; fixtures support one successful check-in path and one deterministic failure path (e.g. expired QR token).
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins for enrolled student A with valid qrToken within present window; query check_in_attempts for student A and session.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins for enrolled student B with expired qrToken; query check_in_attempts for student B and session.
Verify AttendanceRecord exists only for student A success path; confirm no success row for student B failure path.
cases.expected: Attempt/audit coverage row satisfied: every terminal check-in submission persists exactly one CheckInAttempt with structured outcome (Success or rejection code), non-null submittedAt, student and session foreign keys, and token reference per FR-22 and BR-23.

cases.title: M04 check-in transaction persists success CheckInAttempt with token reference and links AttendanceRecord.source_attempt_id
preconditions: Real database transaction is enabled for M04 Check-in module write ownership of check_in_attempts.
Open session with valid rotating QR token exists; enrolled student has no AttendanceRecord for the session.
Test harness can capture User-Agent header and optional GPS payload on submission.
steps: Submit check-in command with valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, gps payload, and known User-Agent within present window.
Query check_in_attempts for outcome, qr_session_token_id, submitted_at, client_timestamp, gps fields, device_user_agent, and correlation_id.
Query attendance_records for the student-session pair and inspect source_attempt_id, check_in_method, and check_in_at.
cases.expected: Success path inserts one CheckInAttempt with outcome Success, non-null qr_session_token_id, ISO-8601 submitted_at, persisted GPS coordinates and gps_validation_result when supplied, device_user_agent, and correlation_id; AttendanceRecord.source_attempt_id references the attempt id with check_in_method QR per FR-22.

cases.title: SM-04 terminal failure persists CheckInAttempt before short-circuit without attendance mutation
preconditions: Integration database seeded with Closed session and enrolled student with no success AttendanceRecord.
Validation order can reach SessionClosed terminal state at step 2 per API §5.1.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord row count and status for student-session pair.
Submit check-in command against Closed session with previously issued qrToken.
Capture afterState for CheckInAttempt.outcome, submitted_at, and AttendanceRecord rows.
cases.expected: SM-04 evaluation terminates at SessionClosed; afterState shows one persisted CheckInAttempt with outcome SessionClosed and non-null submitted_at; AMI-02 holds with AttendanceRecord identical to beforeState.

cases.title: GPS-required failure persists optional GPS validation summary on CheckInAttempt
preconditions: Effective policy for class section requires GPS validation with configured room radius.
Open session exists; enrolled student has no success AttendanceRecord.
Test harness can supply GPS coordinates outside allowed radius with accuracyMeters within acceptable threshold.
steps: Submit check-in command with gps payload placing student outside configured radius.
Query check_in_attempts for outcome, gps_latitude, gps_longitude, gps_accuracy_meters, distance_from_room_meters, and gps_validation_result.
Query attendance_records to confirm no Present/Late row was created.
cases.expected: Failed OutOfRadius path persists one CheckInAttempt with structured outcome OutOfRadius, populated GPS coordinate fields, distance_from_room_meters, gps_validation_result Fail, and submitted_at timestamp; no AttendanceRecord success mutation per FR-22 optional GPS validation result requirement.

cases.title: Concurrent distinct check-in submissions each insert separate CheckInAttempt rows
preconditions: Open session with valid rotating QR tokens exists.
Two enrolled students A and B have no success AttendanceRecord for the session.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests with distinct Idempotency-Key values.
steps: Submit concurrent POST /v1/check-ins for student A and student B with valid tokens.
Query check_in_attempts filtered by class_session_id and count rows per student.
Verify each success path has matching AttendanceRecord with source_attempt_id linkage.
cases.expected: Each concurrent submission persists its own CheckInAttempt with terminal outcome and timestamp; no attempt row is dropped or merged; successful attempts link to distinct AttendanceRecord rows via source_attempt_id.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins 200 success envelope correlates with persisted Success CheckInAttempt
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated enrolled student bearer token is available.
Request includes Idempotency-Key and X-Request-Id headers.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP 200 response envelope data.outcome Success, data.attendanceStatus, and meta.requestId.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt by correlation_id or requestId and compare outcome, submitted_at, and qr_session_token_id.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 200 with standard envelope where data.outcome is Success and error is null; persisted CheckInAttempt.outcome equals Success with matching correlation_id and non-null submitted_at per API §4.4 transaction boundary step 4.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins 422 error.code maps one-to-one to persisted CheckInAttempt outcome
preconditions: Open class session with rotating QR token exists in test environment.
Authenticated enrolled student bearer token is available.
Test clock can advance beyond token expiresAt.
steps: Advance clock past qr.expiresAt for the current session token.
Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, expired qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP status, response envelope error.code, and meta.requestId.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt for the request correlation id and compare outcome field.
cases.expected: API returns 422 with standard envelope where data is null and error.code is ExpiredQr; persisted CheckInAttempt.outcome equals ExpiredQr with non-null submitted_at per FR-22 100% failed-attempt reason coverage.

cases.title: WF-03 primary workflow logs success CheckInAttempt after auth login and check-in submission
preconditions: Lecturer has opened session; rotating valid QR token is displayed.
Unauthenticated enrolled student account exists with known credentials.
Student has no AttendanceRecord for the session.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/auth/login and receives accessToken with Student role per FR-15/FR-36.
Student calls GET /v1/me to confirm studentUserId identity.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with Bearer token, valid qrToken, and clientTimestamp within present window.
Query check_in_attempts and attendance_records for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: WF-03 happy path persists one CheckInAttempt with outcome Success, token reference, submitted_at, and correlation metadata; AttendanceRecord is created with source_attempt_id linking to the attempt row and Present or Late status per FR-22 and FR-23.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance reflects latestAttemptOutcome from persisted attempts
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with Authorization bearer token.
Open session has enrolled student with one failed ExpiredQr CheckInAttempt and another student with Success attempt.
dispute-trail fixture set seeds attempt rows with known outcome codes.
steps: Student submits POST /v1/check-ins producing ExpiredQr failure; record attempt id and outcome.
Lecturer calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Authorization header.
Inspect HTTP 200, data.counts.rejectedAttempts, and data.rows[].latestAttemptOutcome for affected students.
Cross-check roster latestAttemptOutcome values against check_in_attempts.outcome for the same studentUserId.
cases.expected: Roster returns HTTP 200 with stable envelope per FR-19; rejectedAttempts count and per-row latestAttemptOutcome match persisted CheckInAttempt outcome codes without exposing attempts outside lecturer scope.

cases.title: Documented failure outcome matrix achieves 100% CheckInAttempt persistence with structured reason codes
preconditions: Test matrix covers canonical outcomes: ExpiredQr, SessionNotOpen, SessionClosed, NotEnrolled, DuplicateCheckIn, GpsRequired, GpsDisabled, OutOfRadius, LowAccuracy.
Each row has a deterministic fixture to reach that terminal outcome without bypassing validation order.
steps: For each documented failure outcome code, trigger POST /v1/check-ins and capture HTTP status (422/409/401 as applicable) and error.code.
Query check_in_attempts filtered by studentUserId and classSessionId for each triggered failure.
Aggregate results against NFR-13 minimum evidence: 100% failed attempts include structured reason code.
cases.expected: Every matrix failure path returns documented error.code in the API envelope and persists exactly one CheckInAttempt with the same structured outcome and non-null submitted_at; zero terminal failures lack a persisted attempt row per FR-22.

cases.title: CheckInAttempt.read scope denies cross-student and out-of-scope attempt visibility
preconditions: Student A has a failed ExpiredQr CheckInAttempt on session SA in section assigned to Lecturer L1.
Student B is enrolled in a different section; Lecturer L2 is assigned only to Student B section.
ITAdmin identity exists per permission matrix with Deny by default on CheckInAttempt.read.
steps: Student B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionIdA}/attendance and inspects response for Student A attempt metadata.
Lecturer L2 calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionIdA}/attendance for session outside assigned scope.
ITAdmin calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionIdA}/attendance without explicit academic grant.
Lecturer L1 (control) calls the same roster endpoint for assigned session SA.
cases.expected: Student B, Lecturer L2, and ITAdmin receive 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope without disclosing Student A CheckInAttempt details; Lecturer L1 control sees scoped rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome derived from persisted attempts for assigned section only.

cases.title: UI journey maps API failure reason code to CheckInResultScreen state aligned with persisted attempt
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser at PG-02 check-in page.
Open session exists; test can supply an expired QR token via deep link query parameter.
Lecturer is logged in on desktop at PG-06 live roster for the same session.
steps: Student navigates to /check-in?token=<expired> and submits check-in.
Observe CheckInResultScreen renders failure-expired-qr danger alert with localized copy (e.g. 'Mã QR đã hết hạn') and retry CTA per 12-ui-states §5.1.
Query or verify via API that a CheckInAttempt with outcome ExpiredQr was persisted for the submission.
Lecturer refreshes PG-06 roster and inspects rejectedAttempts count and student row latestAttemptOutcome badge.
cases.expected: Student UI maps API ExpiredQr to failure-expired-qr state with recoverable retry CTA; visible failure corresponds to one persisted CheckInAttempt with matching structured outcome; lecturer roster shows incremented rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome ExpiredQr per FR-22 feedback semantics.
